title: 10 best herbal teas for bloating
intro_desc: Herbal teas for bloating are infusions made from various plants and herbs that are known to help reduce bloating and promote digestive health. They work by helping the body to eliminate excess gas and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Some examples of herbal teas that are commonly used for bloating include peppermint, ginger, chamomile, and fennel. These teas can be enjoyed either hot or cold and can be consumed before or after meals. By reducing bloating, herbal teas can help individuals to feel more comfortable in their daily lives, allowing them to engage in activities without discomfort or pain. They can also help to improve overall digestion, leading to better nutrient absorption and overall gut health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with bloating because it contains menthol, a natural compound that relaxes the gastrointestinal muscles and allows gas to pass through the digestive system more easily. This can help to alleviate the discomfort and fullness associated with bloating. Additionally, peppermint has carminative properties, which means it can help to reduce the formation of gas in the first place. Furthermore, peppermint tea is caffeine-free and can help to promote hydration, which is important for overall digestive health. Drinking peppermint tea can be a simple and effective way to help combat bloating and promote a more comfortable digestive experience.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Peppermint tea helps relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing bloating and discomfort caused by spasms. This property is due to the active constituent, menthol.
Carminative: Peppermint tea promotes the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, alleviating bloating and associated pain. The primary constituent responsible for this effect is menthol.
Analgesic: Peppermint tea has pain-relieving properties that can help reduce the discomfort and pain associated with bloating. This is attributed to the presence of menthol and flavonoids.

Relaxant: Peppermint tea has a calming effect on the gastrointestinal tract, which can help alleviate bloating caused by stress or anxiety. The active constituents responsible for this effect are rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
Digestive stimulant: Peppermint tea promotes the production of digestive enzymes, improving overall digestion and reducing bloating. The primary constituent responsible for this effect is menthol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant are rich in volatile oils, including menthol, which helps to relax the gastrointestinal muscles and relieve bloating.
Stems: The stems of the peppermint plant contain similar medicinal properties as the leaves, and can help to alleviate symptoms of bloating and promote digestion.
Flowers: The flowers of the peppermint plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce bloating and discomfort in the abdomen.
Seeds: The seeds of the peppermint plant are a good source of essential oils, which can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce bloating.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the hot water for 5-10 minutes to allow the beneficial compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the spent peppermint leaves, ensuring a smooth and enjoyable drinking experience.
Optional: Add a touch of honey or lemon to enhance the flavor and soothe your digestive system.
Sip the herbal peppermint tea slowly, allowing it to gently alleviate bloating and promote overall digestive health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with bloating because it is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the gut. It also contains a type of catechin called EGCG, which can help to improve gut health by promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria. Additionally, white tea is naturally low in caffeine and has a mild flavor, making it a gentle and soothing drink for those experiencing bloating and discomfort. Its natural diuretic properties can also help to reduce fluid retention, further alleviating the symptoms of bloating.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate bloating. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.
Antispasmodic: White tea has antispasmodic properties that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, preventing cramping and bloating. This is due to the presence of volatile oils.

Diuretic: White tea has diuretic properties that can help increase urine production, which can help eliminate excess fluid and reduce bloating. This is due to the presence of caffeine and epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG).

Antibacterial: White tea has antibacterial properties that can help maintain a healthy balance of bacteria in the gut, preventing the overgrowth of harmful bacteria that can cause bloating. This is due to the presence of polyphenols.
Carminative: White tea has carminative properties that can help relieve gas and bloating by promoting the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract. This is due to the presence of volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate bloating.
Buds: Rich in flavonoids and catechins, which can help improve digestion and reduce symptoms of bloating.
Stems: Can help stimulate the digestive system and reduce bloating due to their diuretic properties.
Flowers: Contain volatile oils that can help relax the gastrointestinal tract and reduce bloating and gas.
Roots: May have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate bloating and discomfort in the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down to 175-185°F (80-85°C), as boiling water can damage the tea's delicate properties.
Add 1 teaspoon (2-3 grams) of high-quality organic white tea leaves to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the hot water over the tea leaves and let them steep for 3-5 minutes, depending on the desired strength.
Remove the tea leaves and optionally add 1/2 inch (1 cm) of fresh ginger root or 1/2 teaspoon (1 grams) of fennel seeds for added bloating relief.
Drink the herbal white tea 2-3 times a day, 30 minutes before meals, to help alleviate bloating symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with bloating because it has carminative properties that allow it to relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce bloating, gas, and discomfort. It contains antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can also alleviate bloating. Furthermore, lemon balm has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to digestive problems and bloating. Drinking lemon balm tea may help to soothe the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Lemon balm tea can help to relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing bloating and allowing for the expulsion of excess gas. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as citronellal and citral.

Antispasmodic: Lemon balm tea can help to alleviate intestinal spasms, which can contribute to bloating and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this effect include rosmarinic acid and eugenol.

Diuretic: Lemon balm tea can help to promote urine production, which can assist in the elimination of excess fluids and reduce bloating. The diuretic effect of lemon balm tea is attributed to its polyphenolic compounds.

Anxiolytic: Lemon balm tea has calming and relaxing properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, both of which can contribute to bloating. The anxiolytic effect of lemon balm tea is due to its rosmarinic acid content.

Antimicrobial: Lemon balm tea can help to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, which can contribute to bloating and other digestive symptoms. The antimicrobial properties of lemon balm tea are due to its volatile oils and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are widely used in making medicinal tea for bloating due to their carminative properties that help in reducing bloating and gas.

Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant are used in making medicinal tea for bloating as they contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate bloating.

Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant are used in making medicinal tea for bloating as they contain rosmarinic acid, which has antispasmodic properties that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce bloating.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a minute or two, so it's not boiling hot when added to the lemon balm.
Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried lemon balm leaves or 4 teaspoons of fresh lemon balm leaves.
Place the lemon balm leaves in a tea infuser or teapot and pour the hot water over the leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on how strong you like the flavor.
Remove the lemon balm leaves and enjoy the herbal tea while it's still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena tea helps with bloating because it has natural diuretic properties that can help your body eliminate excess fluid and reduce bloating. It also contains antispasmodic agents that can help soothe and relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the likelihood of gas and bloating. Additionally, verbena tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate inflammation in the digestive tract, further reducing bloating and discomfort. The tea's soothing and calming effects can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to digestive issues and bloating. Overall, verbena tea is a natural and effective way to relieve bloating and promote digestive health.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Verbena tea helps to relieve bloating by acting as a carminative, which allows it to expel gas from the stomach and intestines, thus reducing the sensation of bloating. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as citral and verbascoside.

Diuretic: Verbena tea has diuretic properties that help to reduce bloating by increasing urine production, which aids in the removal of excess fluids and waste products from the body. This is attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides like aucubin.

Anti-inflammatory: Verbena tea's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate bloating by reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to feelings of bloating and discomfort. This is due to the presence of verbascoside and other phenolic compounds.

Antispasmodic: Verbena tea can help soothe abdominal muscles and reduce bloating by acting as an antispasmodic, which helps to relax the gastrointestinal tract and alleviate cramping and spasms. This is due to the presence of verbascoside and other iridoid glycosides.

Digestive aid: Verbena tea aids digestion, which can help prevent bloating caused by poor digestion or gastrointestinal issues. This is due to the presence of various volatile oils, flavonoids, and iridoid glycosides that stimulate digestive enzymes and promote healthy digestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the verbena tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate bloating and promote digestion.

Flowers: The flowers of the verbena tea plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and help with digestion, making them a popular choice for medicinal tea to treat bloating.

Seeds: The seeds of the verbena tea plant are rich in essential oils and antioxidants, which can help soothe the digestive system and reduce bloating, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water to a rolling boil for 1-2 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried verbena leaves to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the verbena leaves and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the herbal verbena tea up to three times a day between meals for bloating relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with bloating because it contains a compound called anethole, which has been shown to have antispasmodic properties, helping to relax the gastrointestinal tract and reduce bloating. Additionally, fennel is known to stimulate the production of gastric juices and bile, aiding in digestion and the prevention of gas buildup in the stomach. Furthermore, fennel's natural anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties can help reduce fluid retention, further alleviating bloating and discomfort. Its soothing and mildly sweet flavor makes it an enjoyable and natural remedy for promoting healthy digestion.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel tea can help to relieve bloating by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, allowing gas to pass more easily and reducing the feeling of fullness (active constituents
Diuretic: Fennel tea can help to reduce bloating by promoting the production of urine, which helps to eliminate excess fluid and reduce swelling in the abdomen (active constituents
Antispasmodic: Fennel tea can help to soothe the digestive tract and alleviate bloating by reducing muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract (active constituents
Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea can help to reduce bloating by decreasing inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to feelings of bloating and discomfort (active constituents
Antimicrobial: Fennel tea can help to prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, which can contribute to bloating and other digestive issues (active constituents
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for bloating due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and bloating in the digestive tract.
Leaves: Fennel leaves are used in tea to soothe digestive issues, including bloating, due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Rhyzomes: Fennel rhyzomes are rich in volatile oils, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and alleviate symptoms of bloating.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain flavonoids that have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce bloating and improve digestion.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of crushed fennel seeds to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the fennel seeds.
Drink the herbal fennel tea while warm, up to three times a day for bloating relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tea helps with bloating because it contains volatile oils, such as carvone and limonene, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce bloating and gas. Additionally, caraway tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can improve the breakdown and absorption of food, further reducing the likelihood of bloating. Its anti-spasmodic properties also help to soothe the digestive tract, providing relief from discomfort and pain. Overall, caraway tea can be a natural and effective way to alleviate bloating and promote healthy digestion.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Caraway tea helps to relieve bloating by promoting the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, thanks to its volatile oil content, particularly carvone and limonene.
Antispasmodic: It alleviates bloating by reducing smooth muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to bloating, due to its flavonoid and monoterpene components.
Diuretic: Caraway tea aids in decreasing bloating by increasing urine production, which helps eliminate excess fluids and salts from the body, due to its potassium-rich composition.

Digestive stimulant: It enhances digestion and absorption processes, reducing the likelihood of bloating caused by incomplete digestion or malabsorption, owing to its essential oils and bitter compounds.

Anti-inflammatory: Caraway tea helps soothe inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby alleviating bloating and discomfort, due to its anti-inflammatory agents like flavonoids and polyacetylenes.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for bloating due to their carminative properties that help relieve gas and bloating.
Leaves: The leaves of the caraway tea plant are used in medicinal tea to aid digestion and reduce bloating, thanks to their volatile oils and flavonoids.
Fruits: The fruits of the caraway tea plant, often mistaken for seeds, are rich in essential oils and are used in medicinal tea to alleviate symptoms of bloating and promote gut health.
Rhizomes: Caraway rhizomes contain essential oils and are used in medicinal tea to soothe digestive issues, including bloating and gas.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried caraway seeds and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the caraway seeds.
Allow the tea to steep for 10 minutes to extract the herb's beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the seeds.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day, to help alleviate bloating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with bloating because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate bloating and discomfort. It contains volatile oils, such as bisabolol, that have been shown to relax the smooth muscles of the intestines, potentially reducing bloating caused by gas and cramping. Additionally, chamomile tea is known for its carminative effect, which helps to break up and expel intestinal gas, further reducing the sensation of bloating. Its soothing and calming properties may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Carminative: German chamomile tea helps alleviate bloating by relaxing the smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, allowing trapped gas to be released. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils, particularly chamazulene and bisabolol.

Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile tea reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help relieve bloating and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory effects are attributed to the flavonoids and terpenoids found in chamomile.

Spasmolytic: German chamomile tea can help soothe and relax the intestinal muscles, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to bloating. The spasmolytic properties are attributed to the flavonoid apigenin and its ability to bind to benzodiazepine receptors in the smooth muscle.

Diuretic: Chamomile tea promotes the production of urine, which can help remove excess fluids and reduce bloating. The diuretic effect is due to the presence of potassium nitrate and hydrocarbon derivatives in chamomile.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tea contains antioxidants that help protect the gastrointestinal lining from damage, reducing the risk of inflammation and bloating. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the high levels of phenolic compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, found in chamomile.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in volatile oils and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in reducing bloating and soothing digestive discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, alleviating bloating and promoting proper digestion.
Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a great source of essential oils and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating bloating and promoting gut health.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil fresh water and let it cool slightly to around 200°F (93°C) to avoid damaging the delicate chamomile compounds.
Pour the hot water over the chamomile flowers, submerging them completely, and let them steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the medicinal properties to infuse.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to discard the spent flowers.
Serve the herbal tea hot, optionally adding a drizzle of honey for sweetness, and enjoy up to three times a day to help alleviate bloating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with bloating because it contains active compounds that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the gastrointestinal tract. These compounds, including glycyrrhizin, have been shown to increase the mucus lining of the stomach, which can protect the stomach from acid and help prevent inflammation. Additionally, licorice tea may help stimulate the production of bile, which is needed for digestion and can help break down food in the stomach, reducing the likelihood of bloating and discomfort. Furthermore, licorice tea can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, which can alleviate symptoms of bloating and promote regularity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, which can alleviate bloating and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Demulcent: Licorice tea can create a protective coating on the digestive tract lining, which can help to heal and soothe the gastrointestinal tract, reducing bloating and other symptoms of indigestion. The demulcent property is attributed to the high mucilage content in licorice.
Laxative: Licorice tea can help stimulate bowel movements, which can help to relieve bloating and constipation. The laxative effect is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin.

Antispasmodic: Licorice tea can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, which can alleviate bloating, cramping, and other symptoms of indigestion. This property is due to the presence of various flavonoids and volatile oils.

Carminative: Licorice tea can help to expel gas from the digestive tract, which can help to alleviate bloating and discomfort. The carminative property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as anethole, which can help to reduce gas and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for bloating due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant are used in medicinal tea for bloating as they contain flavonoids that can help relax gastrointestinal muscles and reduce bloating.

Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tea plant are used in medicinal tea for bloating as they contain compounds that can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, aiding in digestion and reducing bloating.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the licorice tea plant are used in medicinal tea for bloating as they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that can help soothe the mucous membranes of the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 8 ounces of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day between meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with bloating because it has natural diuretic properties that can help reduce excess fluid buildup in the body, which can alleviate feelings of bloating and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion tea is known to promote digestion and aid in the breakdown of fats, which can further help to prevent bloating after meals. The tea is also rich in antioxidants and vitamins, including vitamin C and A, which can support overall gut health and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Drinking dandelion tea can be a gentle and natural way to relieve bloating and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion tea increases urine production, helping to eliminate excess fluids and reduce bloating. This is mainly due to the presence of potassium and terpenoids in dandelion.
Carminative property: Dandelion tea can help reduce bloating by expelling gas from the stomach and intestines, thanks to its volatile oils and sesquiterpene lactones.

Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion tea can help alleviate bloating by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to feelings of bloating. This is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds and sesquiterpene lactones.
Prebiotic property: Dandelion tea promotes the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can improve digestion and reduce bloating. This is due to the inulin content in dandelion.

Choleretic property: Dandelion tea stimulates the production of bile, which aids in digestion and the absorption of nutrients, reducing the likelihood of bloating. This is mainly due to the presence of taraxasterol and taraxacin in dandelion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelion are used to make medicinal tea for bloating due to their ability to promote digestion and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are used in tea to alleviate bloating as they have diuretic properties that help eliminate excess fluid in the body.
Flowers: The flowers of dandelion are used in tea to soothe bloating and discomfort in the digestive system, as they have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of fresh dandelion flowers or 1-2 teaspoons of dried dandelion flowers.
Rinse the flowers thoroughly under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the flowers in a teapot and add 1 cup of boiling water.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the flowers, and enjoy 2-3 times a day after meals to alleviate bloating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with bloating because it has carminative properties that allow it to effectively reduce gas and alleviate bloating in the digestive system. This herbal tea is known for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic benefits, which help to decrease bloating by promoting the elimination of excess fluid in the body. Furthermore, yarrow tea stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, enhancing digestion and preventing the accumulation of gases in the gastrointestinal tract. Its soothing effect on the gastrointestinal system makes yarrow tea an excellent natural remedy for bloating and other digestive discomforts.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Yarrow tea helps to relieve bloating by reducing the formation of gas in the gastrointestinal tract, due to the presence of volatile oils such as camphor, limonene, and cineole.
Antispasmodic: Yarrow tea alleviates bloating by relaxing the smooth muscles of the intestines, thus reducing spasms and cramps, thanks to the action of flavonoids like luteolin and apigenin.
Diuretic: Yarrow tea promotes urination, which aids in the removal of excess fluids and waste from the body, thereby reducing bloating, due to the presence of lactones and tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea helps to decrease inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to bloating, due to the anti-inflammatory effects of sesquiterpene lactones like costunolide and dehydrocostus lactone.

Astringent: Yarrow tea helps to tighten and tone the tissues of the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and excess mucus production, which can lead to bloating, due to the tannins present in yarrow.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Yarrow leaves are commonly used in medicinal tea for bloating due to their carminative properties that help relieve gas and bloating.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers are used in tea to alleviate bloating, thanks to their diuretic properties that promote urination and help eliminate excess fluids from the body.
Stems: Yarrow stems contain volatile oils and bitter compounds that aid digestion, making them useful in tea to reduce bloating caused by indigestion.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are rich in essential oils and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them helpful in tea to soothe the digestive tract and alleviate bloating.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow herb to a tea infuser or teapot for 5-10 minutes.
Pour the hot water over the yarrow herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the herbal yarrow tea up to three times a day, between meals, for bloating relief.
